children.value: This guide walks you through creating your first meshing and CFD project in Gradient Dynamics Studio. By the end, you’ll have generated a mesh around a geometry and run a simple aerodynamic simulation.
Open Gradient Dynamics Studio in your browser and sign in with your account. You’ll land on the
Give your project a name (e.g., “My First Mesh”)
You’ll be taken to the project workspace.
Select your CAD file — supported formats are:
Your geometry will appear in the 3D viewer
STEP files preserve the most geometric detail (faces, edges, topology). Use STEP whenever possible for the best meshing results.
tab. Studio automatically analyzes your geometry for:
— Are there non-manifold edges or vertices?
If issues are found, use the
options (vertex welding, hole filling) or ask the AI Assistant for help.
— Flow around an object (vehicles, aircraft, buildings)
— Flow inside a geometry (pipes, ducts, enclosures)
domains, a bounding box automatically appears around your geometry in the 3D viewer. Adjust the padding distances (upstream, downstream, sides, top) or use the defaults.
Base cell size in the domain
Boundary layers are critical for capturing near-wall flow physics:
to determine the correct first layer height for your flow conditions. For standard RANS with k-ω SST, target y+ ≈ 30.
tab, add refinement zones to capture flow features:
— Rectangular region (good for wakes, ground effects)
— Cylindrical region (good for rotating zones, jet flows)
— Spherical region (good for point sources, probes)
Drag and resize zones directly in the 3D viewer or enter exact coordinates.
tab, assign names to boundary patches:
Click on a surface in the 3D viewer to select it
These names carry through to your exported mesh and simulation setup
The job is submitted to cloud GPUs
Once complete, the mesh appears in the 3D viewer
tab — check skewness, aspect ratio, and non-orthogonality histograms.
tab (OpenFOAM, Fluent, CGNS, VTU, Gmsh, Nastran)
to run a simulation using this mesh
Return to the Dashboard and click
Select the mesh you generated (or upload one)
— Select k-ω SST (recommended for most external flows)
— Studio auto-detects boundaries from your named surfaces:
Walls are automatically set as no-slip
— SIMPLE algorithm with default relaxation factors works for most cases
— the credit cost estimate is shown before you confirm
Watch for residuals dropping below your convergence criteria
— Color boundaries by pressure, velocity, or turbulence fields
— Cut through the domain to see internal flow structure
— Trace flow paths through the domain
— Calculate drag, lift, and moment coefficients
panel and ask questions in natural language:
“Set up an external aerodynamics mesh for this car geometry”
“What boundary layer settings should I use for highway speed?”
“Run a simulation at 60 mph with k-omega SST”
The assistant analyzes your geometry, suggests configurations, and can directly apply settings with your confirmation. See
